title: Ethiopian Lentil Stew
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish made with red lentils and spices, served with a side of injera bread.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the lentils and 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Stir in the misir wot (Ethiopian spice mix).
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
Serve with injera bread or over rice.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is made with a combination of spices and chilies, so feel free to adjust the spice level to your liking.
